data.title: Daktronics acquires intellectual property and equipment assets from X Display Company
data.description:
• Daktronics (DAKT) (NASDAQ: DAKT) said on Tuesday it has acquired intellectual property, equipment assets and technical expertise from X Display Company Technology Ltd to expand its capabilities in MicroLED and MicroIC display technologies.
  • The U.S.-based display maker said the acquisition includes proprietary mass-transfer and MicroLED fabrication technologies for display applications, specialized development equipment, and the hiring of 15 former X Display Company employees with expertise in MicroLED, MicroIC and mass-transfer processes.

  • Daktronics did not disclose the financial terms of the transaction.

  • DAKT -0.15% after hours to $18.88.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Xiao-I Corporation receives Nasdaq deficiency notices
data.description:
• Xiao-I Corporation (AIXI) announced on Tuesday that it has received two written notifications from Nasdaq, indicating that the company is not in compliance with certain continued listing requirements for the Nasdaq Global Market.
  • On December 16, 2025, the company received a notice from Nasdaq stating that the company does not currently satisfy a minimum closing bid price of $1.00 per share.
  • The notice was based on the company's American Depositary Shares having a closing bid price below $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days from November 3, 2025, through December 15, 2025. In accordance with Nasdaq Listing Rules, the company has been provided a 180-calendar-day compliance period, expiring on June 16, 2026, to regain compliance. 
  • On December 17, 2025, the company received a second notice from Nasdaq stating that the company does not currently satisfy the minimum market value of publicly held shares requirement of at least $15 million. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: First Quantum to sell Las Cruces mine in Spain for up to $190 mln
data.description:
• First Quantum Minerals (FM:CA) on Tuesday said it has agreed to sell its Las Cruces mine in Spain for consideration of up to $190 million, plus a profitability-linked earn-out.
  • The sale will be made through its wholly owned subsidiary, Cobre Las Cruces, to Global Panduro, a company controlled by funds managed by Resource Capital Funds.
  • Consideration includes $45 million in cash at closing, an approximately $65 million loan note, and up to $80 million in deferred payments tied to development milestones, along with a contingent earn-out linked to future exit and liquidity events.
  • First Quantum said the transaction is subject to customary conditions and regulatory approvals and is expected to close in the first half of 2026.
  • FM:CA closed +2.38% at $36.95.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Birchtech approves 1-for-5 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Birchtech (BCHT:CA) said on Tuesday its board has approved a 1-for-5 reverse stock split of its common shares, effective December 26, 2025.
  • The company said its shares are expected to begin trading on a split-adjusted basis at the market open on December 31, while the trading symbol will remain unchanged.
  • On the OTCQB market, a temporary “D” will be added to the ticker for 20 business days following the effective date.
  • Birchtech said the reverse split is intended to increase its share price to support marketability and its planned uplisting strategy.
  • Each five outstanding shares will be consolidated into one share, with fractional shares rounded up to the nearest whole share.
  • BCHT -1.11% after hours to $0.7614.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NXT Energy Solutions acquires complete ownership of SFD Technology
data.description:
• NXT Energy Solutions (SFD:CA) (NSFDF)  announced on Tuesday that it has acquired the remaining rights to the SFD technology previously owned by the heir of its founder and former NXT CEO George Liszicasz.
  • As a result of this transaction, NXT now holds full ownership of the SFD technology across all present and future applications, sensor uses, and geophysical targets, including, but not limited to mineral systems and other strategic subsurface resources.
  • Prior to this acquisition, NXT held ownership of the SFD technology for hydrocarbon and geothermal applications.
  • The transaction was completed on arm's-length terms, the company said. "The heir of George Liszicasz remains a significant shareholder of NXT."
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Four Corners Property Trust acquires Crash Champions property for $2.6 million
data.description:
• Four Corners Property Trust (FCPT) announced on Tuesday the acquisition of a Crash Champions property for $2.6 million.
  • The property is located in a retail corridor in Ohio and is corporate-operated under a long term, net lease with approximately nine years of term remaining, the company said. 
  • The transaction was priced at a 7.2% cap rate including rent credits received at closing and exclusive of transaction costs.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rocket is the top mortgage finance services stock in terms of YTD performance
data.description:
As 2025 draws to a close, Wall Street has observed another strong year of gains across most sectors. 

The financial sector has emerged as the fourth best-performing one YTD, with an advance of +14.73%. Its accompanying State Street Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLF) is +14.69% YTD.

Seeking Alpha took a look at the financials subsector that covers companies providing commercial and residential mortgage finance services and identified the top YTD performers. Rocket (RKT) grabbed the number one spot with a +70.8% gain. 

Here is the list:

  1. Rocket (RKT), Rating: Hold 3.43, +70.8% YTD.
  2. PennyMac Financial Services (PFSI), Rating: Hold 3.24, +31.9% YTD.
  3. MGIC Investment (MTG), Rating: Strong Buy 4.53, +25.1% YTD.
  4. Enact (ACT), Rating: Hold 2.95, +24.6% YTD.
  5. Essent (ESNT), Rating: Hold 2.80, +21% YTD.
  6. Radian (RDN), Rating: Hold 3.31, +15.2% YTD.
  7. NMI (NMIH), Rating: Hold 3.18, +12.6% YTD.
  8. UWM (UWMC), Rating: Hold 2.90, -17% YTD.
  9. Walker & Dunlop (WD), Rating: Sell 2.34, -36.7% YTD.

Here are some financial sector-focused exchange-traded funds: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO).

data.title: Can-Fite BioPharma approves 1-for-3,000 reverse share split; shares down
data.description:
• Can-Fite BioPharma’s (CANF) board approved a 1-for-3,000 reverse split of its ordinary shares following shareholder approval on November 10, 2025.
  • The reverse split will take effect on the Tel-Aviv Stock Exchange on January 2, 2026, with post-split trading beginning January 5, 2026.
  • Concurrently, the ADS-to-ordinary share ratio will change from 1 ADS representing 300 shares to 1 ADS representing 2 shares, effective January 5, 2026, on NYSE American.
  • Authorized shares will decrease from 42B to 14M.
  • CANF shares fall 22% post-market on Tuesday.

data.title: Theralase(R) closes $1.3M non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Theralase® Technologies (TLTFF) on Tuesday closed a non-brokered private placement of units, issuing 7.85M units at C$0.17 per unit for gross proceeds of approximately C$1.33M.
  • Each unit consists of one common share and one common share purchase warrant, with each warrant exercisable at C$0.21 per share for five years from the date of closing.
  • The net proceeds are expected to be used to further the company’s Phase II NMIBC clinical program, along with working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The company paid finder’s fees of $31,658, including $15,470 in cash and $16,188 in shares, and issued 186,221 non-transferable finder warrants exercisable at $0.21 per share for five years from closing.
  • Shares +1.74%.

data.title: Hagerty is the best performing insurance broker YTD
data.description:
The S&P 500 financials sector has largely kept pace with the broader market in 2025, rising about 14% year to date.

Financials rank as the fourth-best performing sector in the S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the broader financial sector, below is a list of insurance broker stocks ranked by their YTD performance. The list includes each company’s SA Quant rating.

The list is topped by Hagerty (HGTY), with a YTD performance of +40.52% and a Strong Buy Quant Rating of 4.82. Willis Towers Watson Public (WTW) comes in second with a 6.12% YTD gain, followed by Aon plc (AON) at -1.03%.

The remaining stocks on the list exhibit negative YTD performance. Arthur J. Gallagher (AJG), Marsh & McLennan Companies (MMC), and Ryan Specialty (RYAN) occupy the middle positions. The Baldwin Insurance Group (BWIN) shows the lowest performance at -38.36% with a Strong Sell Quant Rating of 1.39.

Here is the list:

  1. Hagerty (HGTY), YTD perf: +40.52%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.82)

  2. Willis Towers Watson (WTW), YTD perf: +6.12%, Quant Rating: Hold (2.97)

  3. Aon plc (AON), YTD perf: -1.03%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.00)

  4. Arthur J. Gallagher (AJG), YTD perf: -9.02%, Quant Rating: Sell (1.97)

  5. Marsh & McLennan Companies (MMC), YTD perf: -12.66%, Quant Rating: Hold (2.96)

  6. Ryan Specialty Holdings (RYAN), YTD perf: -18.94%, Quant Rating: Sell (1.60)

  7. Brown & Brown (BRO), YTD perf: -21.22%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.03)

  8. Goosehead Insurance (GSHD), YTD perf: -30.05%, Quant Rating: Sell (1.83)

  9. The Baldwin Insurance Group (BWIN), YTD perf: -38.36%, Quant Rating: Strong Sell (1.39)

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO)
